user_input: Thank you. Literally can't see the halo at all, no matter how much I adjust . Is the halo somehow black? it looks like the coastline borders don't show up or show up black perhaps. Also, in the "What Am I looking at?" Menu, can we replace the label "Colored Sparks Feature packets" with "Arcs - Predictions" and then get rid of the endtire "Arc dots - prediction routes section and subtiles please?

content:
Fixing invisible border halo rendering and updating the explain-panel copy. Inspecting how coastlines and colors are implemented.
Fog was blending the light coast ribbons into the dark background. Disabling fog on coast materials, raising and brightening the halo layers, and updating the explain-panel copy.
Fog was blending the light coast ribbons into the dark background. Disabling fog on coast materials, raising and brightening the halo layers, and updating the explain-panel copy.
Fixed two issues in `lightgbm-emissions-art-v2.html` and the React export.

### Why the halo looked black / invisible
The coast ribbons used light cream colors, but **scene fog** (`FogExp2` tinted with the dark background `#0B0D10`) was applied to those meshes. Fog pulled them toward near-black, so even at max tweak strength they barely showed.

**Fix:**
- `fog: false` on all coast border materials
- Raised ribbons above the land fill (y ≈ 0.14–0.22 vs land at 0.018)
- **CAT yellow** outer halo (`#FFB000`) with **additive blending** on the two widest layers
- Thicker ribbons (~2× width) and default **3.0×** (slider now **0.5–8×**)

You should see a warm yellow-white glow along the US outline; crank **Tweaks → US border halo** to **5–6×** if you want it bolder.

### “What am I looking at?” copy
| Change |
|--------|
| **Colored sparks — feature packets** → **Arcs — Predictions** |
| Removed the whole **Arc dots — prediction routes** row |

The explain panel now has three items: Ensemble Core, Leaf nodes, and Arcs — Predictions.

Refresh the preview (standalone, not `?embed=1`, so **Tweaks** stays visible) and confirm the coast glow reads clearly. If it’s still faint on your display, say so and we can push the yellow layer further or add a screen-space outline pass.Fixed two issues in `lightgbm-emissions-art-v2.html` and the React export.

### Why the halo looked black / invisible
The coast ribbons used light cream colors, but **scene fog** (`FogExp2` tinted with the dark background `#0B0D10`) was applied to those meshes. Fog pulled them toward near-black, so even at max tweak strength they barely showed.

**Fix:**
- `fog: false` on all coast border materials
- Raised ribbons above the land fill (y ≈ 0.14–0.22 vs land at 0.018)
- **CAT yellow** outer halo (`#FFB000`) with **additive blending** on the two widest layers
- Thicker ribbons (~2× width) and default **3.0×** (slider now **0.5–8×**)

You should see a warm yellow-white glow along the US outline; crank **Tweaks → US border halo** to **5–6×** if you want it bolder.

### “What am I looking at?” copy
| Change |
|--------|
| **Colored sparks — feature packets** → **Arcs — Predictions** |
| Removed the whole **Arc dots — prediction routes** row |

The explain panel now has three items: Ensemble Core, Leaf nodes, and Arcs — Predictions.

Refresh the preview (standalone, not `?embed=1`, so **Tweaks** stays visible) and confirm the coast glow reads clearly. If it’s still faint on your display, say so and we can push the yellow layer further or add a screen-space outline pass.
